understanding.goal_understanding: Progress through KA59’s levels by positioning all selectable pieces so that every goal tile marked with a '+' is occupied at the same time; completing the final level ends the run with WIN, within each level’s step budget.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: The currently controlled piece is the one shown as 'P' (and identified by player.id/position). MOVE actions shift that selected piece one grid cell (3 pixels) in the chosen direction unless blocked/altered by boundaries or objects. If a pushable object (e.g., a block) is directly adjacent in the movement direction, the move can push it along as the player moves, provided space permits. Walls/boundaries can stop movement and may have non-uniform blocking behavior that requires experimentation. If there are multiple selectables, you switch control by CLICKing the exact pixel/grid position of another selectable (shown as 'S' in the semantic grid and listed in objects.selectables). The movement.*_blocked flags are only hints about adjacency and do not guarantee whether a move will succeed.

history.summary: Turn 1: MOVE_RIGHT | We appear fully enclosed by walls; try moving to test if any wall is passable/acts like a portal. Start by pushing right against the wall.
history.reasoning: We appear fully enclosed by walls; try moving to test if any wall is passable/acts like a portal. Start by pushing right against the wall.

history.summary: Turn 2: MOVE_RIGHT | We’re enclosed by walls; try pushing into the right wall to see if it’s passable/teleporting or triggers a shift.
history.reasoning: We’re enclosed by walls; try pushing into the right wall to see if it’s passable/teleporting or triggers a shift.

history.summary: Turn 3: MOVE_RIGHT | We’re boxed in, but moving right may push into/through the adjacent selectable and advance toward the goal area.
history.reasoning: We’re boxed in, but moving right may push into/through the adjacent selectable and advance toward the goal area.

history.summary: Turn 4: CLICK | We’re boxed in by walls on all sides with this piece; switch to the other selectable to try reaching the goal area.
history.reasoning: We’re boxed in by walls on all sides with this piece; switch to the other selectable to try reaching the goal area.

history.summary: Turn 5: MOVE_RIGHT | Move onto the adjacent goal tile to occupy it and trigger the win condition (only one goal region exists).
history.reasoning: Move onto the adjacent goal tile to occupy it and trigger the win condition (only one goal region exists).

history.summary: Turn 6: MOVE_LEFT | Move onto the central goal tile so the selectable occupies the goal area for the win check.
history.reasoning: Move onto the central goal tile so the selectable occupies the goal area for the win check.

history.summary: Turn 7: MOVE_RIGHT | Move onto the adjacent goal area to satisfy the win condition for this single-goal level.
history.reasoning: Move onto the adjacent goal area to satisfy the win condition for this single-goal level.

history.summary: Turn 8: MOVE_RIGHT | The goal area is directly adjacent; try stepping onto the right-side goal cell since goals are non-collidable and may allow overlap despite the wall hint.
history.reasoning: The goal area is directly adjacent; try stepping onto the right-side goal cell since goals are non-collidable and may allow overlap despite the wall hint.

history.summary: Turn 9: MOVE_LEFT | Move onto the goal cluster by stepping left into the adjacent goal tile (goals are non-collidable).
history.reasoning: Move onto the goal cluster by stepping left into the adjacent goal tile (goals are non-collidable).

history.summary: Turn 10: MOVE_RIGHT | Try moving onto the adjacent goal tile to the right; goals are non-collidable so this should place the selectable correctly.
history.reasoning: Try moving onto the adjacent goal tile to the right; goals are non-collidable so this should place the selectable correctly.

history.summary: Turn 11: MOVE_LEFT | We need the selected piece to occupy the goal area; move left onto the adjacent goal tile (it’s non-collidable) despite the wall hint.
history.reasoning: We need the selected piece to occupy the goal area; move left onto the adjacent goal tile (it’s non-collidable) despite the wall hint.

history.summary: Turn 12: MOVE_LEFT | We need to occupy the only goal tile; try moving onto the adjacent goal cell to the left (blocked hint is unreliable since goals are non-collidable).
history.reasoning: We need to occupy the only goal tile; try moving onto the adjacent goal cell to the left (blocked hint is unreliable since goals are non-collidable).

history.summary: Turn 13: MOVE_UP | We need the selected piece to occupy the goal region; moving up should place it onto the adjacent goal tiles area despite the wall hint.
history.reasoning: We need the selected piece to occupy the goal region; moving up should place it onto the adjacent goal tiles area despite the wall hint.

history.summary: Turn 14: MOVE_RIGHT | Step onto the goal area by moving right into the + tile cluster; goals are non-collidable so this should place the piece correctly.
history.reasoning: Step onto the goal area by moving right into the + tile cluster; goals are non-collidable so this should place the piece correctly.
